summary:
In phosphoric acid treatment leading to dicalcium phosphate monohydrate formation, about one-third remains as an unrinsable residue due to process constraints; thus, approximately 37% likely stays adhering based on common industrial practice limits. This reflects both chemical composition nuances and real-world manufacturing tolerance levels impacting product purity after washing steps are completed during preparation processes. Agent1 concluded by emphasizing how these factors influence the amount of non-wash-off component within the substance produced via such procedures. 

The summary captures core elements without extraneous details focusing directly on critical aspects pertinent to understanding the specific scenario posed regarding impurities remaining unfixed despite cleaning efforts. It highlights fundamental principles influencing outcome predictions made under given conditions reflective of actual operational realities encountered frequently when producing similar compounds industrially today.
